name: Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop
name_original: Puig de ses Torretes — circular desde Santa Eulària des Riu por Cala Llonga
description: This moderate 14.6-mile mountain bike loop around Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga offers technical terrain and coastal views in Ibiza.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Santa Eulalia Del Río offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths with sea views to inland routes through green valleys and wooded hills. The region features a mix of terrain, including paved roads, gravel tracks, and technical single tracks, catering to various skill levels. Pine woodlands are a common feature, and the Santa Eulària River adds a unique riparian element to some routes. This varied topography provides a comprehensive mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Eulalia Del Río The most popular mountain bike trail is Santa Eulària: R5 Beaches Route, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route features technical terrain and offers views from Torre d'en Valls. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puig d'en Cires — circular desde Santa Gertrudis de Fruitera por Sant Mateu d'Eubarca y Canal de Buscastell, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.3 km) path. This route includes significant elevation changes as it winds through rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and technical terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Eulalia Del Río is defined by coastal paths, wooded hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly routes to more challenging technical single tracks. The routes in Santa Eulalia Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Eulalia Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: The trails offer a diverse mix of landscapes, including coastal paths with stunning sea views, hidden coves, and dramatic cliffs. Inland, you'll find serene rural areas with green valleys, wooded hills, and traditional farmlands. The terrain varies from paved roads and gravel stretches to rural paths and technical single tracks, providing a comprehensive mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for all skill levels in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: Yes, Santa Eulalia Del Río caters to all skill levels. The region offers a wide array of routes, from easy, family-friendly options to more challenging technical single tracks for experienced riders. For example, there are 43 easy routes and 12 difficult routes available, alongside 125 moderate trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: Absolutely. The municipality specifically recommends 12 cycling routes designed for families, which are typically of low to medium difficulty. These routes are perfect for exploring the town's heritage and natural resources at a relaxed pace.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can encounter a variety of natural and cultural highlights. Trails often pass through pine woodlands, along the unique Santa Eulària River, and past traditional farmlands. Cultural landmarks include the 16th-century Puig de Missa Church, and you can also find natural attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aigües Blanques Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5847493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Forn&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Santa Eulalia Del Río are circular, allowing for convenient starting and ending points. They are often interconnected, providing flexibility for extended excursions or combining different sections.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005580672/santa-eularia-r5-beaches-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eulària: R5 Beaches Route&lt;/a&gt; offer technical terrain over 22.8 miles. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38205665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antenas Downhill Trail – Santa Gertrudis de Fruitera loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes over a much longer distance.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: With approximately 300 days of sunshine annually, Santa Eulalia Del Río is ideal for outdoor activities year-round. The pleasant weather makes it suitable for mountain biking in most seasons, though spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures for longer rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santa Eulalia Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse coastal paths, wooded hills, and river valleys, as well as the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santa Eulalia Del Río?
A: There are over 180 mountain bike trails available around Santa Eulalia Del Río, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Can I find routes that combine coastal views with inland exploration?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed to showcase both the stunning coastline and the tranquil inland scenery. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010311420/puig-de-ses-torretes-and-cala-llonga-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides both technical terrain and coastal views, while other trails wind through green valleys and traditional farmlands.

Q: Are there any cultural sites or attractions I can visit while mountain biking?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. The region features landmarks such as the 16th-century Puig de Missa Church and the Ethnological Museum. Some routes, like the Sant Carles BTT Route, pass by cultural sites such as the Church of Sant Carles and the Fuente de Peralta.

Q: What are some moderate mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009263060/puig-d-en-cires-circular-desde-santa-gertrudis-de-fruitera-por-sant-mateu-d-eubarca-y-canal-de-buscastell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cires — circular desde Santa Gertrudis de Fruitera&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.6 miles with significant elevation changes. Another good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009253815/sant-carles-de-peralta-and-puig-des-fornas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Carles de Peralta and Puig des Fornas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 17-mile route with panoramic views.
tips: Mostly S1, but in a few places almost S3. At the summit there is a Steinberg instead of a summit cross. Driving from Santa Eulalia to Cala Llonga, the climb at the very end is pretty steep for the last 100 m, so I had to push briefly. After the summit, there is a challenging descent over a sometimes very stony narrow single trail, here too I had to descend briefly at one point. Still very nice, at the end down to Cala Llonga the trail then becomes a forest road. Coming in the other direction from Cala Llonga, the single trail from the forest road is not ridable, at least with an organic bike.
From up here the views of the south-east coast of Ibiza are great .... Cala Llonga on one side and Sol d'en Serrà on the other

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered moderate to challenging for mountain biking. It demands good technique and strength due to its technical terrain, which includes steep sections with scree. While some parts might start on tarmac, it quickly transitions to dirt paths with challenging climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and can be quite technical. You'll encounter steep sections with scree, dry and fast singletracks, and challenging climbs and descents. The route transitions from potential tarmac starts to dirt paths, winding through coastal areas, pine forests, and hilly interiors.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning views of Ibiza's south-east coast, including Cala Llonga and Sol d'en Serrà. You'll find panoramic vistas of Tagomago Island and Formentera from the ruins of a Phoenician settlement at Cap des Llibrell. Don't miss the Highest View of Cala Llonga and the Ses Torretes Peak, both offering fantastic photo opportunities.

faqs.question: Is the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't have specific restrictions against dogs, given the technical and challenging nature of the mountain biking route with steep sections and scree, it might not be suitable for all dogs, especially if they are accompanying a rider. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, on a leash, and capable of handling the terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop. It's an open trail accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this loop is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potential for heavier rains in winter. This makes for more comfortable riding conditions on the technical terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the exact trailhead is not provided, but Cala Llonga itself is a well-known area with public parking options. You can typically find parking near the beach or in designated areas within Cala Llonga, which serves as a good starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or notable landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant historical point of interest is the ancient Phoenician settlement ruins at Cap des Llibrell, which also offers panoramic views. Additionally, you'll pass by geodetic markers at Puig de Ses Torretes (220m) and Puig des Castellar (184m), which serve as notable landmarks.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with official trails such as El Camí IT4TR5 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, Cala Llonga, Puig de ses Torretes, and Ruta Platges, among others. This offers opportunities to explore different parts of the area if you wish to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Puig de ses Torretes and Cala Llonga Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is approximately 23.5 kilometers long with nearly 600 meters of elevation gain. For an average mountain biker, it typically takes around 2 hours and 6 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as 'moderate to challenging' and the presence of steep sections with scree requiring good technique and strength, this loop is generally not recommended for beginner mountain bikers. Riders should have a good level of fitness and technical skill to safely and enjoyably navigate the terrain.
